forbiddenClaims: that a blocked or uninspected route (e.g., an authenticated admin or draft-content route) was observed
reproduction of the source site's proprietary copy, wordmark, exact palette, unique illustrations, or distinctive iconography
an invented documentation route, fabricated API reference, or speculative content structure not present in the snapshot
a cross-origin link treated as part of the migrated surface without clear marking
affiliation with any real documentation-platform vendor observed or implied by the source

rubricAnchors.score1: The adaptation ignores the inspected documentation information architecture or invents routes/API references not present in the snapshot.
rubricAnchors.score3: The adaptation follows the inspected IA but adds an unsupported route or mislabels a blocked route as inspected.
rubricAnchors.score5: The adaptation preserves the inspected documentation IA for developer and technical-writer users, with no claim relying on a blocked or uninspected route.

rubricAnchors.score1: Docs-home and article-route share no common navigation, heading hierarchy, or code-block treatment.
rubricAnchors.score3: Navigation and heading hierarchy are retained on some routes but drift between docs-home and article-route.
rubricAnchors.score5: A single direction system unifies docs-home and article-route, including a consistent left-rail navigation, heading hierarchy, and code-block treatment as generic principles.

rubricAnchors.score1: No actionable blueprint; engineers cannot infer the search-active, nav-collapsed-mobile, code-block-copied, or route-not-found states.
rubricAnchors.score3: Partial blueprints with unresolved nav-collapsed-mobile or route-not-found states.
rubricAnchors.score5: Engineers can build docs-home and article-route directly, including all declared states, the operable mobile nav drawer, and the code-block horizontal-scroll-in-container rule.

rubricAnchors.score1: A verbatim copy of the source site's copy, wordmark, palette, illustrations, or iconography.
rubricAnchors.score3: Generic structural principles adapted but a proprietary identifier (wordmark, copy, illustration, palette, or icon) slips through.
rubricAnchors.score5: Only generic structural principles are retained or adapted; every proprietary identifier is explicitly rejected with a recorded retain/adapt/reject decision.

rubricAnchors.score1: Keyboard navigation regresses (e.g., nav drawer not keyboard-reachable) and no route-not-found or search-active state handling.
rubricAnchors.score3: Keyboard handling preserved on some elements but the mobile nav drawer or search-active state is inconsistent.
rubricAnchors.score5: The collapsed mobile nav drawer remains keyboard-reachable, focus is preserved across navigation, and route-not-found and search-active states are handled explicitly.

rubricAnchors.score1: Claims rely on a blocked route, an uninspected page, or an invented API reference.
rubricAnchors.score3: Most claims trace to inspected routes but one claim overreaches into a blocked or uninspected route.
rubricAnchors.score5: Every claim traces to an inspected route in the snapshot and blocked/uninspected routes are explicitly excluded.

adjudicationNotes: retain/adapt/reject lanes are all permitted: generic structural principles may be retained or adapted, while proprietary identifiers must be rejected.
A blocked route (e.g., an authenticated admin or draft-content route) MUST be treated as not-observed; any claim relying on it fails evidence-discipline.
Same-origin internal links between inspected routes are load-bearing for documentation wayfinding; breaking them or treating cross-origin links as migrated content fails product-appropriateness.
Originality and evidence-discipline are co-equal here: copying proprietary elements OR claiming uninspected pages fails regardless of other strengths.

The brief's sourceSnapshotRef.sha256 binds the exact on-disk snapshot bytes; the manifest generator verifies this binding on every build (placeholder sha will be replaced once the real snapshot is authored).
